title: gentiana acuta subsp. plebeia
intro: Gentiana acuta subsp. plebeia, also known as the "Tall Gentian," is a species of flowering plant in the Gentianaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as gentiopicrin and gentianine. The medicinal properties of Gentiana acuta subsp. plebeia have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Gentiana acuta subsp. plebeia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine practices to promote overall well-being.
